meaning: adj.平易近人的,友好的n.伙伴,好朋友
examples: She started off being quite matey with everyone.
Frost had displayed his usual matey charm as the pair sipped orange juice on national TV.
The matey relation between the doctors and patients has active effect on preventing medical disputes.
Pope Francis appears to be what Argentinians would say is "campechano" - approachable, "matey", even.
The other was being treated at Ochsner Medical Center in the New Orleans suburb of Kenner, Matey said.
